krv.TIT_1_1: 하나님의 종이요 예수 그리스도의 사도인 바울 곧 나의 사도 된 것은 하나님의 택하신 자들의 믿음과 경건함에 속한 진리의 지식과
krv.TIT_1_2: 영생의 소망을 인함이라 이 영생은 거짓이 없으신 하나님이 영원한 때 전부터 약속하신 것인데
krv.TIT_1_3: 자기 때에 자기의 말씀을 전도로 나타내셨으니 이 전도는 우리 구주 하나님의 명대로 내게 맡기신 것이라
krv.TIT_1_4: 같은 믿음을 따라 된 나의 참 아들 디도에게 편지하노니 하나님 아버지와 그리스도 예수 우리 구주로 좇아 은혜와 평강이 네게 있을지어다
krv.TIT_1_5: 내가 너를 그레데에 떨어뜨려 둔 이유는 부족한 일을 바로잡고 나의 명한 대로 각 성에 장로들을 세우게 하려 함이니
krv.TIT_1_6: 책망할 것이 없고 한 아내의 남편이며 방탕하다 하는 비방이나 불순종하는 일이 없는 믿는 자녀를 둔 자라야 할지라
krv.TIT_1_7: 감독은 하나님의 청지기로서 책망할 것이 없고 제 고집대로 하지 아니하며 급히 분내지 아니하며 술을 즐기지 아니하며 구타하지 아니하며 더러운 이를 탐하지 아니하며
krv.TIT_1_8: 오직 나그네를 대접하며 선을 좋아하며 근신하며 의로우며 거룩하며 절제하며
krv.TIT_1_9: 미쁜 말씀의 가르침을 그대로 지켜야 하리니 이는 능히 바른 교훈으로 권면하고 거스려 말하는 자들을 책망하게 하려 함이라
krv.TIT_1_10: 복종치 아니하고 헛된 말을 하며 속이는 자가 많은 중 특별히 할례당 가운데 심하니
krv.TIT_1_11: 저희의 입을 막을 것이라 이런 자들이 더러운 이를 취하려고 마땅치 아니한 것을 가르쳐 집들을 온통 엎드러치는도다
krv.TIT_1_12: 그레데인 중에 어떤 선지자가 말하되 그레데인들은 항상 거짓말장이며 악한 짐승이며 배만 위하는 게으름장이라 하니
krv.TIT_1_13: 이 증거가 참되도다 그러므로 네가 저희를 엄히 꾸짖으라 이는 저희로 하여금 믿음을 온전케 하고
krv.TIT_1_14: 유대인의 허탄한 이야기와 진리를 배반하는 사람들의 명령을 좇지 않게 하려 함이라
krv.TIT_1_15: 깨끗한 자들에게는 모든 것이 깨끗하나 더럽고 믿지 아니하는 자들에게는 아무 것도 깨끗한 것이 없고 오직 저희 마음과 양심이 더러운지라
krv.TIT_1_16: 저희가 하나님을 시인하나 행위로는 부인하니 가증한 자요 복종치 아니하는 자요 모든 선한 일을 버리는 자니라
krv.TIT_2_1: 오직 너는 바른 교훈에 합한 것을 말하여
krv.TIT_2_2: 늙은 남자로는 절제하며 경건하며 근신하며 믿음과 사랑과 인내함에 온전케 하고
krv.TIT_2_3: 늙은 여자로는 이와 같이 행실이 거룩하며 참소치 말며 많은 술의 종이 되지 말며 선한 것을 가르치는 자들이 되고
krv.TIT_2_4: 저들로 젊은 여자들을 교훈하되 그 남편과 자녀를 사랑하며
krv.TIT_2_5: 근신하며 순전하며 집안 일을 하며 선하며 자기 남편에게 복종하게 하라 이는 하나님의 말씀이 훼방을 받지 않게 하려 함이니라
krv.TIT_2_6: 너는 이와 같이 젊은 남자들을 권면하여 근신하게 하되
krv.TIT_2_7: 범사에 네 자신으로 선한 일의 본을 보여 교훈의 부패치 아니함과 경건함과
krv.TIT_2_8: 책망할 것이 없는 바른 말을 하게 하라 이는 대적하는 자로 하여금 부끄러워 우리를 악하다 할 것이 없게 하려 함이라
krv.TIT_2_9: 종들로는 자기 상전들에게 범사에 순종하여 기쁘게 하고 거스려 말하지 말며
krv.TIT_2_10: 떼어 먹지 말고 오직 선한 충성을 다하게 하라 이는 범사에 우리 구주 하나님의 교훈을 빛나게 하려 함이라
krv.TIT_2_11: 모든 사람에게 구원을 주시는 하나님의 은혜가 나타나
krv.TIT_2_12: 우리를 양육하시되 경건치 않은 것과 이 세상 정욕을 다 버리고 근신함과 의로움과 경건함으로 이 세상에 살고
krv.TIT_2_13: 복스러운 소망과 우리의 크신 하나님 구주 예수 그리스도의 영광이 나타나심을 기다리게 하셨으니
krv.TIT_2_14: 그가 우리를 대신하여 자신을 주심은 모든 불법에서 우리를 구속하시고 우리를 깨끗하게 하사 선한 일에 열심하는 친 백성이 되게 하려 하심이니라
krv.TIT_2_15: 너는 이것을 말하고 권면하며 모든 권위로 책망하여 누구에게든지 업신여김을 받지 말라
krv.TIT_3_1: 너는 저희로 하여금 정사와 권세잡은 자들에게 복종하며 순종하며 모든 선한 일 행하기를 예비하게 하며
krv.TIT_3_2: 아무도 훼방하지 말며 다투지 말며 관용하며 범사에 온유함을 모든 사람에게 나타낼 것을 기억하게 하라
krv.TIT_3_3: 우리도 전에는 어리석은 자요 순종치 아니한 자요 속은 자요 각색 정욕과 행락에 종 노릇 한 자요 악독과 투기로 지낸 자요 가증스러운 자요 피차 미워한 자이었으나
krv.TIT_3_4: 우리 구주 하나님의 자비와 사람 사랑하심을 나타내실 때에
krv.TIT_3_5: 우리를 구원하시되 우리의 행한 바 의로운 행위로 말미암지 아니하고 오직 그의 긍휼하심을 좇아 중생의 씻음과 성령의 새롭게 하심으로 하셨나니
krv.TIT_3_6: 성령을 우리 구주 예수 그리스도로 말미암아 우리에게 풍성히 부어 주사
krv.TIT_3_7: 우리로 저의 은혜를 힘입어 의롭다 하심을 얻어 영생의 소망을 따라 후사가 되게 하려 하심이라
krv.TIT_3_8: 이 말이 미쁘도다 원컨대 네가 이 여러 것에 대하여 굳세게 말하라 이는 하나님을 믿는 자들로 하여금 조심하여 선한 일을 힘쓰게 하려 함이라 이것은 아름다우며 사람들에게 유익하니라
krv.TIT_3_9: 그러나 어리석은 변론과 족보 이야기와 분쟁과 율법에 대한 다툼을 피하라 이것은 무익한 것이요 헛된 것이니라
krv.TIT_3_10: 이단에 속한 사람을 한두 번 훈계한 후에 멀리 하라
krv.TIT_3_11: 이러한 사람은 네가 아는 바와 같이 부패하여서 스스로 정죄한 자로서 죄를 짓느니라
krv.TIT_3_12: 내가 아데마나 두기고를 네게 보내리니 그 때에 네가 급히 니고볼리로 내게 오라 내가 거기서 과동하기로 작정하였노라
krv.TIT_3_13: 교법사 세나와 및 아볼로를 급히 먼저 보내어 저희로 궁핍함이 없게 하고
krv.TIT_3_14: 또 우리 사람들도 열매 없는 자가 되지 않게 하기 위하여 필요한 것을 예비하는 좋은 일에 힘쓰기를 배우게 하라
krv.TIT_3_15: 나와 함께 있는 자가 다 네게 문안하니 믿음 안에서 우리를 사랑하는 자들에게 너도 문안하라 은혜가 너희 무리에게 있을지어다

kjv.TIT_1_1: Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God's elect, and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness;
kjv.TIT_1_2: In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie, promised before the world began;
kjv.TIT_1_3: But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;
kjv.TIT_1_4: To Titus, mine own son after the common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.
kjv.TIT_1_5: For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting, and ordain elders in every city, as I had appointed thee:
kjv.TIT_1_6: If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of riot or unruly.
kjv.TIT_1_7: For a bishop must be blameless, as the steward of God; not selfwilled, not soon angry, not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre;
kjv.TIT_1_8: But a lover of hospitality, a lover of good men, sober, just, holy, temperate;
kjv.TIT_1_9: Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to convince the gainsayers.
kjv.TIT_1_10: For there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers, specially they of the circumcision:
kjv.TIT_1_11: Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for filthy lucre's sake.
kjv.TIT_1_12: One of themselves, even a prophet of their own, said, the Cretians are alway liars, evil beasts, slow bellies.
kjv.TIT_1_13: This witness is true. Wherefore rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith;
kjv.TIT_1_14: Not giving heed to Jewish fables, and commandments of men, that turn from the truth.
kjv.TIT_1_15: Unto the pure all things are pure: but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their mind and conscience is defiled.
kjv.TIT_1_16: They profess that they know God; but in works they deny him, being abominable, and disobedient, and unto every good work reprobate.
kjv.TIT_2_1: But speak thou the things which become sound doctrine:
kjv.TIT_2_2: That the aged men be sober, grave, temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience.
kjv.TIT_2_3: The aged women likewise, that they be in behaviour as becometh holiness, not false accusers, not given to much wine, teachers of good things;
kjv.TIT_2_4: That they may teach the young women to be sober, to love their husbands, to love their children,
kjv.TIT_2_5: To be discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.
kjv.TIT_2_6: Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded.
kjv.TIT_2_7: In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine shewing uncorruptness, gravity, sincerity,
kjv.TIT_2_8: Sound speech, that cannot be condemned; that he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you.
kjv.TIT_2_9: Exhort servants to be obedient unto their own masters, and to please them well in all things; not answering again;
kjv.TIT_2_10: Not purloining, but shewing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.
kjv.TIT_2_11: For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,
kjv.TIT_2_12: Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;
kjv.TIT_2_13: Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;
kjv.TIT_2_14: Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify unto himself a peculiar people, zealous of good works.
kjv.TIT_2_15: These things speak, and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no man despise thee.
kjv.TIT_3_1: Put them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to every good work,
kjv.TIT_3_2: To speak evil of no man, to be no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all meekness unto all men.
kjv.TIT_3_3: For we ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.
kjv.TIT_3_4: But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,
kjv.TIT_3_5: Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;
kjv.TIT_3_6: Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;
kjv.TIT_3_7: That being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
kjv.TIT_3_8: This is a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable unto men.
kjv.TIT_3_9: But avoid foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.
kjv.TIT_3_10: A man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject;
kjv.TIT_3_11: Knowing that he that is such is subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself.
kjv.TIT_3_12: When I shall send Artemas unto thee, or Tychicus, be diligent to come unto me to Nicopolis: for I have determined there to winter.
kjv.TIT_3_13: Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them.
kjv.TIT_3_14: And let our's also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they be not unfruitful.
kjv.TIT_3_15: All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.

net.TIT_1_1: From Paul, a slave of God and apostle of Jesus Christ, to further the faith of God’s chosen ones and the knowledge of the truth that is in keeping with godliness,
net.TIT_1_2: in hope of eternal life, which God, who does not lie, promised before time began.
net.TIT_1_3: But now in his own time he has made his message evident through the preaching I was entrusted with according to the command of God our Savior.
net.TIT_1_4: To Titus, my genuine son in a common faith. Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior! Titus’ Task on Crete
net.TIT_1_5: The reason I left you in Crete was to set in order the remaining matters and to appoint elders in every town, as I directed you.
net.TIT_1_6: An elder must be blameless, the husband of one wife, with faithful children who cannot be charged with dissipation or rebellion.
net.TIT_1_7: For the overseer must be blameless as one entrusted with God’s work, not arrogant, not prone to anger, not a drunkard, not violent, not greedy for gain.
net.TIT_1_8: Instead he must be hospitable, devoted to what is good, sensible, upright, devout, and self-controlled.
net.TIT_1_9: He must hold firmly to the faithful message as it has been taught, so that he will be able to give exhortation in such healthy teaching and correct those who speak against it.
net.TIT_1_10: For there are many rebellious people, idle talkers, and deceivers, especially those with Jewish connections,
net.TIT_1_11: who must be silenced because they mislead whole families by teaching for dishonest gain what ought not to be taught.
net.TIT_1_12: A certain one of them, in fact, one of their own prophets, said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons.”
net.TIT_1_13: Such testimony is true. For this reason rebuke them sharply that they may be healthy in the faith
net.TIT_1_14: and not pay attention to Jewish myths and commands of people who reject the truth.
net.TIT_1_15: All is pure to those who are pure. But to those who are corrupt and unbelieving, nothing is pure, but both their minds and consciences are corrupted.
net.TIT_1_16: They profess to know God but with their deeds they deny him, since they are detestable, disobedient, and unfit for any good deed.
net.TIT_2_1: But as for you, communicate the behavior that goes with sound teaching.
net.TIT_2_2: Older men are to be temperate, dignified, self-controlled, sound in faith, in love, and in endurance.
net.TIT_2_3: Older women likewise are to exhibit behavior fitting for those who are holy, not slandering, not slaves to excessive drinking, but teaching what is good.
net.TIT_2_4: In this way they will train the younger women to love their husbands, to love their children,
net.TIT_2_5: to be self-controlled, pure, fulfilling their duties at home, kind, being subject to their own husbands, so that the message of God may not be discredited.
net.TIT_2_6: Encourage younger men likewise to be self-controlled,
net.TIT_2_7: showing yourself to be an example of good works in every way. In your teaching show integrity, dignity,
net.TIT_2_8: and a sound message that cannot be criticized, so that any opponent will be at a loss, because he has nothing evil to say about us.
net.TIT_2_9: Slaves are to be subject to their own masters in everything, to do what is wanted and not talk back,
net.TIT_2_10: not pilfering, but showing all good faith, in order to bring credit to the teaching of God our Savior in everything.
net.TIT_2_11: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all people.
net.TIT_2_12: It trains us to reject godless ways and worldly desires and to live self-controlled, upright, and godly lives in the present age,
net.TIT_2_13: as we wait for the happy fulfillment of our hope in the glorious appearing of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ.
net.TIT_2_14: He gave himself for us to set us free from every kind of lawlessness and to purify for himself a people who are truly his, who are eager to do good.
net.TIT_2_15: So communicate these things with the sort of exhortation or rebuke that carries full authority. Don’t let anyone look down on you.
net.TIT_3_1: Remind them to be subject to rulers and authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work.
net.TIT_3_2: They must not slander anyone, but be peaceable, gentle, showing complete courtesy to all people.
net.TIT_3_3: For we too were once foolish, disobedient, misled, enslaved to various passions and desires, spending our lives in evil and envy, hateful and hating one another.
net.TIT_3_4: But “when the kindness of God our Savior and his love for mankind appeared,
net.TIT_3_5: he saved us not by works of righteousness that we have done but on the basis of his mercy, through the washing of the new birth and the renewing of the Holy Spirit,
net.TIT_3_6: whom he poured out on us in full measure through Jesus Christ our Savior.
net.TIT_3_7: And so, since we have been justified by his grace, we become heirs with the confident expectation of eternal life.” Summary of the Letter
net.TIT_3_8: This saying is trustworthy, and I want you to insist on such truths, so that those who have placed their faith in God may be intent on engaging in good works. These things are good and beneficial for all people.
net.TIT_3_9: But avoid foolish controversies, genealogies, quarrels, and fights about the law, because they are useless and empty.
net.TIT_3_10: Reject a divisive person after one or two warnings.
net.TIT_3_11: You know that such a person is twisted by sin and is conscious of it himself. Final Instructions and Greeting
net.TIT_3_12: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to spend the winter there.
net.TIT_3_13: Make every effort to help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way; make sure they have what they need.
net.TIT_3_14: Here is another way that our people can learn to engage in good works to meet pressing needs and so not be unfruitful.
net.TIT_3_15: Everyone with me greets you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all.
